understanding.project_summary: This project provides AutoDocX: a FastAPI service and supporting tools that scan a local Python project, extract developer-reference data, generate an LLM-assisted structured understanding, and produce standalone HTML developer documentation. It includes scanners, parsers, analyzers, LLM provider integrations, persistent storage for scans/understandings/documents, and a Streamlit UI client that calls the API.
understanding.architecture_overview: AutoDocX is layered into (1) an HTTP API layer (app.main, app.api.project_routes) that exposes project operations; (2) scanning & analysis services (app.services.project_scanner, app.services.code_parser, app.services.project_analyzer) that produce a detailed project scan; (3) context and LLM orchestration (app.services.project_context_builder, app.services.project_understanding_service, app.services.llm.*) which build LLM-ready context, call a provider, and validate/merge the output; (4) persistence components (app.services.scan_storage, app.services.understanding_storage, app.services.document_storage) that save/retrieve scans, understandings, and generated HTML; and (5) presentation (app.services.document_builder, ui.streamlit_app) that build and surface documentation. At runtime requests flow from API handlers into service objects: scans are created by the scanner (which uses the parser and analyzer), stored by ScanStorage, optionally compared by ScanComparator, transformed to a compact context, passed to an LLM provider via the provider factory, validated and merged, then rendered to HTML and saved by DocumentStorage. Configuration and workspace paths are provided by app.core.config.

execution_flow.title: Receive API request
execution_flow.description: A FastAPI route in app.api.project_routes receives an HTTP request (examples: /api/projects/scan or /api/projects/documentation/sync) and validates the Pydantic request model from app.models.project.

execution_flow.title: Scan files and parse Python source
execution_flow.description: ProjectScanner enumerates files, computes file hashes and sizes, and delegates Python file parsing to PythonCodeParser to extract imports, symbols, routes, signatures, docstrings and source previews.

execution_flow.title: Analyze project relationships
execution_flow.description: ProjectAnalyzer consumes parsed files to build module maps, discover FastAPI routers/routes, internal dependencies, symbols and project-level statistics used for documentation.

execution_flow.title: Persist scan and optionally compare
execution_flow.description: ScanStorage saves the scan JSON under workspace/scans. When updating documentation, ScanComparator compares two stored scans to produce a summary of file/symbol/route/dependency changes.

execution_flow.title: Build LLM-ready context and request understanding
execution_flow.description: ProjectContextBuilder compacts the stored scan into 'detailed' or 'llm' modes. ProjectUnderstandingService uses the LLM provider factory and prompts to request a structured narrative, retrying if required and merging reliable parser/analyzer data into the LLM output.

execution_flow.title: Render and store HTML documentation
execution_flow.description: DocumentBuilder converts the validated stored understanding into a complete HTML document (API reference, module reference, execution flow, risks). DocumentStorage writes the HTML and metadata to workspace/documents and returns document metadata to the API response.

classes.name: PythonCodeParser
classes.docstring:
Reads a Python file and extracts developer-documentation-ready
structure from it.

Extracted information:
- imports
- module constants
- functions
- async functions
- classes
- class attributes
- class methods
- function/method signatures
- arguments with annotations/defaults
- return annotations
- docstrings
- decorators
- FastAPI routes
- source previews
- content hashes
- syntax errors

constants.name: PROJECT_UNDERSTANDING_SYSTEM_PROMPT
constants.value:
'
You are AutoDocX, a professional developer documentation engine.

You receive deterministic project context extracted from source code.
The context may include modules, classes, functions, method signatures,
FastAPI routes, dependencies, docstrings, and project statistics.

Your job is to generate clean developer documentation metadata.

Important rules:

1. Do not invent files, functions, classes, APIs, databases, services,
   dependencies, parameters, return values, or behavior.
2. Keep module names, function names, class names, method names, and API
   paths exactly as supplied.
3. Write like professional developer docs, not like an audit report.
4. Do not write phrases like "Confirmed", "Reasonable inference",
   "Based on the context", or "It appears".
5. If something is unclear, write a short neutral sentence such as:
   "The provided scan does not expose this detail."
6. Do not include Markdown code fences.
7. Do not include API keys, credentials, secrets, or environment values.
8. Return exactly one valid JSON object.
9. Do not return text before or after the JSON object.

The JSON object must follow this exact structure:

{
  "project_summary": "string",
  "architecture_overview": "string",
  "execution_flow": [
    {
      "step": 1,
      "title": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "related_modules": ["string"]
    }
  ],
  "module_responsibilities": [
    {
      "module": "string",
      "file": "string",
      "responsibility": "string",
      "important_symbols": ["string"]
    }
  ],
  "api_overview": [
    {
      "method": "string",
      "path": "string",
      "handler": "string",
      "purpose": "string"
    }
  ],
  "key_dependencies": [
    {
      "source": "string",
      "target": "string",
      "purpose": "string"
    }
  ],
  "risks_and_gaps": [
    {
      "title": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "severity": "low | medium | high"
    }
  ],
  "recommended_document_sections": [
    {
      "title": "string",
      "purpose": "string"
    }
  ]
}

Style requirements:

- project_summary should be 1 to 2 concise paragraphs.
- architecture_overview should explain the application layers and how
  code moves through them.
- execution_flow should describe runtime flow in practical developer terms.
- module_responsibilities should be short and specific.
- api_overview should explain what each endpoint is used for.
- risks_and_gaps should focus on real engineering gaps such as
  authentication, error handling, retries, persistence, testing,
  configuration, observability, and deployment readiness.
- recommended_document_sections should suggest useful developer docs,
  not generic business report sections.
'.strip()

risks_and_gaps.title: No API authentication/authorization
risks_and_gaps.description: API surface (project_routes) does not show any authentication or authorization. Endpoints that trigger LLM calls, file system scans, and storage operations are unprotected.

risks_and_gaps.title: LLM API key management and secrecy
risks_and_gaps.description: LLM provider keys are read via app.core.config helpers but the repository does not document secure provisioning, rotation, or runtime secrets management for provider credentials.

risks_and_gaps.title: Persistence durability and concurrency
risks_and_gaps.description: Storages (scans, understandings, documents) write JSON and HTML to workspace paths. There is no exposed backing DB, versioning beyond filenames, or explicit concurrency control for simultaneous requests.

risks_and_gaps.title: Limited observability and error instrumentation
risks_and_gaps.description: No explicit logging, metrics, or tracing integration is visible in the scanned modules; diagnosing failures or LLM issues in production will be difficult without structured logs/metrics.

risks_and_gaps.title: Testing and validation coverage not shown
risks_and_gaps.description: The scan does not expose unit/integration tests or test harnesses for critical components (parser, comparator, LLM handling).

risks_and_gaps.title: Retry and rate-limit handling for LLM providers
risks_and_gaps.description: ProjectUnderstandingService implements a single retry for malformed JSON, but there is no configurable exponential backoff, rate-limiting, or provider-specific error handling visible for transient LLM errors.

risks_and_gaps.title: Input validation and large project handling
risks_and_gaps.description: Large projects may produce large scans/contexts. The code does not show chunking/size limits or safeguards to prevent oversized LLM prompts or memory exhaustion.

risks_and_gaps.title: Deployment and configuration guidance missing
risks_and_gaps.description: Repository configuration and deployment (environment, workspace mounts, persistent storage) are not documented; containerization, scaling and recommended runtime settings are not exposed.

recommended_document_sections.title: Quickstart — Run locally
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Show step-by-step instructions to install dependencies, configure environment variables (LLM provider/key/model), create workspace directories, and start the FastAPI service and Streamlit UI.

recommended_document_sections.title: API Reference
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document every endpoint (method, path, request/response models, examples) so integrators can call scan, sync, compare, understand, and document endpoints programmatically.

recommended_document_sections.title: Storage layout and persistence
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Explain workspace layout (scans, understandings, documents), file naming, retention/versioning, and guidance on migrating storage to a managed backend or shared volume.

recommended_document_sections.title: LLM Providers and Secrets
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document supported providers (openai, anthropic), provider aliases, required environment variables, secure key management, and recommended provider/model selection.

recommended_document_sections.title: Failure modes & troubleshooting
recommended_document_sections.purpose: List common errors (LLM malformed responses, missing scan files, missing document), how to interpret logs, and recovery steps including re-scanning and re-generating understandings.

recommended_document_sections.title: Testing guide
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Provide instructions and examples for unit and integration tests for parser, analyzer, comparator, and LLM handling, including sample fixtures and expected outputs.

recommended_document_sections.title: Observability and production hardening
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Recommend logging, metrics, tracing, health checks, concurrency limits, and deployment notes (containers, storage volumes, secrets, autoscaling).

recommended_document_sections.title: Contribution and extensibility
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Describe how to add new LLM providers, extend the code parser for additional languages, modify document templates, and the expected interfaces for service components.

classes.docstring:
Reads a Python file and extracts developer-documentation-ready
structure from it.

Extracted information:

- module constants
- functions
- async functions
- classes
- class attributes
- class methods
- function/method signatures
- arguments with annotations/defaults
- return annotations
- docstrings
- decorators
- FastAPI routes
- source previews
- content hashes
- syntax errors

constants.name: PROJECT_UNDERSTANDING_SYSTEM_PROMPT
constants.value:
'
You are AutoDocX, a professional developer documentation engine.

You receive deterministic project context extracted from source code.
The context may include modules, classes, functions, method signatures,
FastAPI routes, dependencies, docstrings, and project statistics.

Your job is to generate clean developer documentation metadata.

Important rules:

1. Do not invent files, functions, classes, APIs, databases, services,
   dependencies, parameters, return values, or behavior.
2. Keep module names, function names, class names, method names, and API
   paths exactly as supplied.
3. Write like professional developer docs, not like an audit report.
4. Do not write phrases like "Confirmed", "Reasonable inference",
   "Based on the context", or "It appears".
5. If something is unclear, write a short neutral sentence such as:
   "The provided scan does not expose this detail."
6. Do not include Markdown code fences.
7. Do not include API keys, credentials, secrets, or environment values.
8. Return exactly one valid JSON object.
9. Do not return text before or after the JSON object.

The JSON object must follow this exact structure:

{
  "project_summary": "string",
  "architecture_overview": "string",
  "execution_flow": [
    {
      "step": 1,
      "title": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "related_modules": ["string"]
    }
  ],
  "module_responsibilities": [
    {
      "module": "string",
      "file": "string",
      "responsibility": "string",
      "important_symbols": ["string"]
    }
  ],
  "api_overview": [
    {
      "method": "string",
      "path": "string",
      "handler": "string",
      "purpose": "string"
    }
  ],
  "key_dependencies": [
    {
      "source": "string",
      "target": "string",
      "purpose": "string"
    }
  ],
  "risks_and_gaps": [
    {
      "title": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "severity": "low | medium | high"
    }
  ],
  "recommended_document_sections": [
    {
      "title": "string",
      "purpose": "string"
    }
  ]
}

Style requirements:

- project_summary should be 1 to 2 concise paragraphs.
- architecture_overview should explain the application layers and how
  code moves through them.
- execution_flow should describe runtime flow in practical developer terms.
- module_responsibilities should be short and specific.
- api_overview should explain what each endpoint is used for.
- risks_and_gaps should focus on real engineering gaps such as
  authentication, error handling, retries, persistence, testing,
  configuration, observability, and deployment readiness.
- recommended_document_sections should suggest useful developer docs,
  not generic business report sections.
'.strip()
